basic_details.job_title: CUSB Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: The latest Central University of South Bihar JRF Recruitment 2026 has been announced! Applications are invited for the position of Junior Research Fellow (JRF) in the Department of Geology under the ANRF-ARG project. This is a 60-month project offering fellowship of Rs. 37,000 + HRA (initial two years) and Rs. 42,000 + HRA (as SRF). Interested candidates can apply within 21 days from the date of notification. Find all details about the CUSB Gaya JRF vacancy 2026, including eligibility, fellowship, and application process here.

important_dates.last_date_to_apply: 21 days from the date of this notification.

qualification.details: criteria: Details | age limit: Not exceeding 28 years as on the date of advertisement (Age relaxation for reserved categories as per GoI norms).
qualification.education_qualification: First class M.Sc./M.Tech. in Geology/Applied Geology + Qualified CSIR-UGC NET/GATE in the relevant subject.

how_to_apply: Interested applicants may send their duly signed filled application form, detailed CV, photocopies of educational degrees, mark sheets, publications, research experience, and CSIR-UGC NET/GATE certificates to [email protected] with the subject “Application for the post of Junior Research Fellow (JRF)” within 21 days from the date of this notification.
Short-listed candidates will be informed by email about the online/offline interview.
important_instructions: The JRF engagement is purely temporary and co-terminus with the project. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the interview. The selected candidate may get an opportunity to register for Ph.D. as per university ordinances.

all_official_links.label: Download Central University of South Bihar JRF Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Central University of South Bihar JRF Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: Department of Geology, Central University of South Bihar, Gaya has invited applications from Indian nationals for the position of Junior Research Fellow (JRF-One) in the ANRF-ARG project (ANRF/ARG/2025/005047/EAS) entitled "An Integrated Study on Fluvial Deposits of River Systems in Western Peninsular India: Insights into Provenance, Depositional Processes and Active Tectonics". Download Central University of South Bihar JRF Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Central University of South Bihar JRF Recruitment 2026 Overview

rows.central_university_of_south_bihar_gaya: An Integrated Study on Fluvial Deposits of River Systems in Western Peninsular India: Insights into Provenance, Depositional Processes and Active Tectonics
Dr. Priti Rai, Assistant Professor, Department of Geology
21 days from the date of this notification.
Rs. 37,000 + HRA (JRF – first two years) Rs. 42,000 + HRA (SRF – subsequent years)

content_sections.heading: Central University of South Bihar JRF Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

rows.details: First class M.Sc./M.Tech. in Geology/Applied Geology + Qualified CSIR-UGC NET/GATE in the relevant subject.
Experience in geological fieldwork, sample preparation/analysis and knowledge of softwares such as ArcGIS, CorelDRAW, Origin, etc.
Not exceeding 28 years as on the date of advertisement (Age relaxation for reserved categories as per GoI norms).

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for CUSB Gaya JRF Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Interested applicants may send their duly signed filled application form, detailed CV, photocopies of educational degrees, mark sheets, publications, research experience, and CSIR-UGC NET/GATE certificates to [email protected] with the subject “ Application for the post of Junior Research Fellow (JRF) ” within 21 days from the date of this notification. Short-listed candidates will be informed by email about the online/offline interview.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: The JRF engagement is purely temporary and co-terminus with the project. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the interview. The selected candidate may get an opportunity to register for Ph.D. as per university ordinances.

faq.question: Q1. What is Central University of South Bihar JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Central University of South Bihar (CUSB), Gaya has invited applications for one Junior Research Fellow (JRF) position in the Department of Geology under the ANRF-ARG funded project on fluvial deposits and active tectonics.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for CUSB Gaya JRF 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must submit their application within 21 days from the date of the notification through email to [email protected].

faq.question: Q3. What is the fellowship amount for JRF at CUSB Gaya?
faq.answer: The fellowship is Rs. 37,000 + HRA per month for the first two years (JRF) and Rs. 42,000 + HRA per month for subsequent years (as SRF) as per ANRF norms.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for CUSB JRF position?
faq.answer: Candidates must have First Class M.Sc./M.Tech. in Geology/Applied Geology and must have qualified CSIR-UGC NET or GATE. Experience in geological fieldwork and relevant software knowledge is desirable. Maximum age limit is 28 years.

faq.question: Q5. How to apply for Junior Research Fellow at Central University of South Bihar?
faq.answer: Candidates should send their filled application form, detailed CV, and supporting documents via email to [email protected] with the subject line “Application for the post of Junior Research Fellow (JRF)”.

faq.question: Q6. Will the selected JRF get a chance to pursue PhD?
faq.answer: Yes, the selected candidate may get an opportunity to join the Ph.D. program in the Department of Geology as per university ordinances.

faq.question: Q7. Is NET/GATE qualification mandatory for CUSB JRF 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates must have qualified CSIR-UGC NET or GATE as per the funding agency norms.

faq.question: Q8. Who is the Principal Investigator for this ANRF project?
faq.answer: Dr. Priti Rai, Assistant Professor, Department of Geology, Central University of South Bihar, Gaya is the Principal Investigator.
